summary: Jeff Atwood argues that as applications become more feature-rich, traditional navigation methods like menus and toolbars fail to scale, making incremental search essential for finding features. He uses Office 2007's ribbon as an example of UI evolution, noting that even this major improvement has limitations when users can't find commands not displayed in the ribbon. Atwood highlights Microsoft's experimental 'Scout' feature and similar implementations in tools like Expression Blend as evidence that incremental feature search is becoming necessary. He concludes that while simple applications can rely on traditional navigation, complex applications need search-based discovery to remain usable.
key_points: Menus and toolbars become unwieldy as applications grow - a flat menu with 8 commands works well, but hierarchical menus with 35+ commands become disasters
Office 2007's ribbon was a major UI innovation but still can't surface all features, leaving users unable to find commands they know exist
Incremental search for application features is becoming as important as incremental search within documents
Microsoft's experimental 'Scout' add-in for Office 2007 demonstrated the value of searchable feature discovery but was killed by internal politics
The pattern of searchable interfaces is spreading across Microsoft products including Vista's start menu and Expression design tools
money_quotes: As applications get larger and more complicated, incremental search is also useful for navigating the sea of features that modern applications offer.

There's a point beyond which menus and toolbars cease to scale well. A flat menu with 8 well-organized commands on it works just great; a three-level hierarchical menu containing 35 loosely-related commands can be a bit of a disaster.
How do you find what isn't in the ribbon?
Applications get more feature-rich over time, and navigation methods have to evolve to keep up.
It's faster to incrementally search for the feature we need.
key_insight: As software complexity inevitably grows, incremental search transforms from a nice-to-have text navigation feature into an essential method for users to discover and access application functionality.
